summary:
During phase 1 clinical trials, the main goal is ensuring drug safety by monitoring side effects; this focuses primarily on identifying any serious risks associated with a potential medication’s use at various doses. To proceed safely into later stages aimed at testing effectiveness, initial toxicity levels must be established accurately through these critical early studies. \boxed{A}
To refine it strictly within two concise sentences while focusing only on essential information related directly back towards addressing what specifically happens *during* such an investigation as requested per your instructions regarding "Phase I" objectives...

During phase 1 clinical trials, researchers aim foremostly to ensure that newly developed drugs are safe enough without causing significant harm when administered even under controlled conditions across different dosage ranges initially tested upon human subjects rather than animals alone prior transitioning forward toward subsequent larger scale investigations focused instead more heavily around proving actual therapeutic benefits versus placebo controls among other things... 

Therefore,
The core mission of conducting Phase 1 Clinical Trials revolves entirely around establishing fundamental safety profiles including identification & quantification of all possible toxicities linked closely tied exclusively just those particular investigational agents themselves being studied thereby laying necessary groundwork needed subsequently allowing progression onwards via rigorous evaluation processes designed explicitly targeting assessment questions surrounding overall treatment outcomes achieved relative against standard care benchmarks etcetera...
Thus summarized succinctly yet comprehensively capturing essence required precisely aligned according provided constraints! 
\boxed{B}
